stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced TODAY (May 5, 2026): consolidated PAT Rs 91.83 cr (+124-125% YoY vs Rs 40.4 cr), net revenue Rs 2,300 cr (+59.8% YoY), Q4 pre-sales Rs 2,039 cr (+11% YoY), Q4 collections Rs 1,989 cr (+26% YoY, +11% QoQ). FY26 pre-sales hit record Rs 8,135-8,140 cr (+30% YoY vs Rs 6,276 cr in FY25); Bengaluru contributed ~55%. Net debt turned negative at ~Rs -800 cr (D/E -0.17). Board recommended dividend of Rs 6/share. Stock up ~3.56% to ~Rs 1,438 in early trade May 5; earnings call scheduled 5:00 PM IST with MD Jagadish Nangineni and CFO Yogesh Bansal.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Realty has been the weakest major Indian sector recently — fell ~16% in 2025 and another ~18% in the month preceding mid-March 2026, with DLF, Oberoi, Lodha hitting 52-week lows. 2026 outlook turning constructive: RBI delivered 100+ bps of cuts in 2025 lowering home-loan EMIs (every 25bps cut reduces a Rs 1 cr/20-yr EMI by ~Rs 1,700/month), and analysts call recent correction a 'healthy reset' with structural demand intact. Bengaluru — where SOBHA derives ~55% of FY26 pre-sales — remains a tech-driven bright spot for residential demand.
stocks.peer_comparison: SOBHA's 1-year return of +17.15% is the standout in the realty basket — DLF -19.09%, Godrej Properties -19.27%, Prestige +7.13%. YTD 2026 SOBHA -6.36% is mid-pack (Prestige +2.3%, DLF +0.71% lead; Godrej trails). Q4 PAT growth (+125%) and FY26 pre-sales growth (+30%) compare favorably to Godrej Properties (Q3 revenue -48.56% YoY) and signal SOBHA executing better than several large peers despite smaller scale.
red_flags: P/E ~79.6x is rich versus sector average and own history — premium pricing leaves limited margin for execution miss
Q3 FY26 standalone net profit had fallen -28.86% YoY, showing earnings volatility quarter-to-quarter
Sector remains in a 12-month downtrend — broad realty sentiment fragile despite SOBHA's individual outperformance
Stock at ~Rs 1,438 is ~17% below 52-week high of Rs 1,732.45, reflecting prior overhang
High geographic concentration in Bengaluru (~55% of FY26 pre-sales)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results today (May 5) — strong PAT and revenue beat already supporting intraday move; conference call 5:00 PM IST may add color on FY27 outlook
Rs 6/share dividend declaration — record date pending
Net debt now negative ~Rs 800 cr — deleveraging story validated
Analyst targets clustered above CMP: Nuvama Rs 1,631 (Buy), MOFSL Rs 1,650, YES Sec Rs 1,615, Emkay Rs 1,560; 14-analyst average Rs 1,878 (~+41% upside)
Continued RBI dovishness and mid-segment housing recovery into FY27
